title: Extract the number from a name (Snowflake REGEXP_SUBSTR)
prompt: You're writing Snowflake SQL. The generic customers are named like "Customer 12". Using Snowflake's REGEXP_SUBSTR, extract the numeric part of each generic customer's name and return it as an integer. Columns: name, num. Order by num.

generic: SELECT name, CAST(regexp_extract(name, '[0-9]+') AS INTEGER) AS num
snowflake: SELECT name, CAST(REGEXP_SUBSTR(name, '[0-9]+') AS INTEGER) AS num

requires.message: Use Snowflake's REGEXP_SUBSTR here, not a different string function.

hints: REGEXP_SUBSTR(name, '[0-9]+') returns the first run of digits in the name.
Wrap it in CAST(… AS INTEGER) so it sorts numerically.
